title: Uniface Messages 4000-4999
text: Uniface Messages 4000-4999 Refer to the Uniface message number, message text, and an explanation of the message. The following table refers to messages with numbering between 4000 - 4999 . Message number Message text Explanation 4100 Your current Uniface repository will be migrated You have objects in the repository that are imported from an older release. These objects will be migrated to match the new repository structure. 4200 Uniface version information copied to message frame When you click any of the hyperlinks on the About Uniface form (for example, Release, Update, Platform, or Date), the information for all four is copied to the message frame. You can copy and paste the information from the message frame, for example, to an email to Uniface support. 4300 Load License failed Your attempt to load the OCX's runtime license failed. Make sure that you have selected the OCX, and that the OCX has a runtime license. Before you try to use runtime OCX licenses, check that the OCX in use supports runtime licenses and that you are allowed to use them. This should be in the documentation of the OCX involved; otherwise the manufacturer of the OCX should be able to provide this information. 4301 Load License successful The OCX runtime license was loaded. 4302 License unloaded The OCX runtime license was unloaded. 4400 The IDE's state has been reset due to an update A new version of the Uniface IDE has been installed which is incompatible with the state saved in the ide_state.zip file. As a consequence, the existing state is dropped and the default (compiled-in) state is used. 4401 The IDE's state for ObjectType : ObjectName has been reset due to an update A new version of the Uniface IDE has been installed which is incompatible with the state of editor for the specified object, as saved in the ide_state.zip file. As a consequence, the existing state is dropped and the default (compiled-in) state is used. 4800 Select a date No date was set or selected for the calendar widget. Click on a date to select it. 4801 Date was out of range. The initial value specified for the calendar date is invalid. Select or provide a correct date. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
